narrative.summary:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Grand St in Williamsburg — James Murphy's wine bar that became one of the world's most respected restaurants.

Michelin-starred. North America's 50 Best. Natural wine list that rewrites the rules. Chef Nick Curtola's seasonal cooking is precise and effortlessly unfussy.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

295 Grand St, Williamsburg — corner spot, unassuming from outside.

You walk in and the first thing you notice is the sound. Not the kitchen, not the conversation — the music. LCD Soundsystem's James Murphy co-founded this place in 2015, and the playlist and sound design are impeccable. Then the wine list arrives and you realize this is one of the most important natural wine programs in America. Then the food comes and you understand why Michelin gave it a star.

blocks.title: Space
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗦𝗣𝗔𝗖𝗘

Warm, minimal, eternally cool without trying. The room is intimate — maybe 40 seats — with a bar you'd happily spend the night at. The late wine director Justin Chearno's spirit lives in every bottle on the shelf. The sound system is probably the best in any restaurant in New York.

blocks.title: Drinks
blocks.body:
𝗗𝗥𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦

🍷 The wine list is the reason this place exists. Eclectic, laser-focused on varietals, predominantly natural. The staff knows every bottle and will guide you with genuine enthusiasm.

• Wines by the glass: ~$16-22
• Bottles: $50-200+ (deep list of natural, biodynamic, and minimal-intervention wines)
• Ask what's just been opened — the by-the-glass pours rotate constantly ⭐

This is a wine bar that also happens to be a Michelin-starred restaurant.

blocks.title: Food
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗨

Chef Nick Curtola's menu changes daily. Seasonal, North Atlantic-sourced, globally accented. Precise and effortlessly unfussy.

𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀 ~$18-22
• Veal sweetbread skewers with soy-cured egg yolk ⭐
• Crisp chickpea crêpe with squash blossoms and glistening lardon ⭐
• Razor clams with fish sauce and lime
• Seasonal crudo

𝗠𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘀 ~$28-42
• Fried skate wing — deep golden, impossibly crisp ⭐
• Berkshire pork with cider glaze
• Chicken schnitzel with blistered sungold tomatoes (seasonal)
• Yakitori-style sweetbreads

𝗗𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘁 ~$15-18
• Crème caramel with toasted crispy soba bits ⭐

𝗕𝗿𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗵 (Fri–Sun from 11am)
Available with a different seasonal menu

Note: Menu changes daily. These are representative dishes — whatever's on when you go will be excellent.

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

💰 $$

Dinner for 2: $140-200 (with wine)

Reservations: Required — book via Resy. Be ready to queue if walk-in.

🌐 fourhorsemenbk.com
📞 (718) 599-4900

𝗛𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗦
Mon–Thu: 5:30–10:45 PM
Fri–Sun: 11:00 AM – 10:45 PM

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦
• Monday/Tuesday dinner: Easiest reservations
• Weekend brunch (11am): Different vibe, more relaxed
• Bar seating: Sometimes available for walk-ins
• Let the staff pick your wine — they're the best in the business at this

discovery.text: Featured in Eater NY — 'Best Wine List in NYC'
